title: This Open Source Repo Just Solved Claude Code's #1 Problem
summary: Graphify is an open source tool that creates knowledge graphs from large code bases without requiring RAG, enabling more accurate and token-efficient responses. It addresses Claude Code's memory constraints by organizing code information into structured graph representations.
writeup: This video demonstrates Graphify, an open-source tool (approaching 60,000 GitHub stars) that converts a code repository into a knowledge graph so that Claude Code can answer architectural questions more accurately and at lower token cost than file-grepping. The three-pass indexing pipeline covers code structure (tree-sitter, no LLM), audio and video (faster-whisper), and documents and images (LLM semantic analysis), producing nodes, edges, and community clusters. A live demo against the Open Design repository (203 files, 1,907 nodes, 3,447 edges, 109 communities) then compares token usage with and without Graphify on identical questions. The through-line is that handing an AI coding agent a pre-built map of a codebase is categorically better than asking it to grep, and Graphify automates building that map.
key_points: Graphify's first pass is purely deterministic: tree-sitter parses source files and extracts classes, functions, imports, call graphs, and inline comments with no LLM involved, making the structural graph free to build and reproducible.
Token savings from using the knowledge graph instead of grepping are real but below the 70x figure some users claim; the demo confirms a meaningful reduction without reaching that ceiling.

The core distinction from GraphRAG systems (LightRAG, Microsoft Graph RAG) is the absence of embeddings: Graphify is purpose-built for code, while GraphRAG handles large collections of unstructured documents that share no inherent call-graph connections.

Installing Graphify also installs a Claude Code skill file that teaches the agent which commands to invoke and when, so users do not need to memorize the full command surface: natural language prompts trigger the right Graphify calls automatically.
The `graphify claude install` command wires Graphify as a permanent hook so every Claude Code session consults the knowledge graph without an explicit per-query instruction.

A single `--obsidian` flag converts the traversed repository into a populated Obsidian vault, giving non-code repositories (pure Markdown or document collections) a second output path beyond the coding-agent use case.
